text: Git branching is a key part of version control that helps teams manage their work. When developers add a new feature, they create a separate feature branch. This allows them to make changes without affecting the main code. Once the feature is complete, they can merge it back into the main branch. Before merging, many teams use pull requests to review the code and discuss any issues. This process creates a clear workflow where each change is tracked and approved. Understanding branches, merging, and pull requests helps teams collaborate more smoothly and avoid conflicts in the project.
